segments.text: It was the day before the Passover festival, and Jesus knew that the time had come to leave this world and go to his Father. He had loved those in the world who were his own, and he now demonstrated his complete love for them.
It was during supper, and the devil had already put the thought of betraying Jesus into the mind of Judas, son of Simon Iscariot.
Jesus knew that the Father had placed everything under his authority,  and that he had come from God and was going back to God.
So Jesus stood up from eating supper, took off his robe and wrapped a towel around his waist.
He poured water into a basin and began to wash his disciples' feet, drying them with the towel he had wrapped around him.
He came to Simon Peter, who asked him, {
Lord, are you going to wash my feet?
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Jesus replied, {
You won't realize what I'm doing for you now. But one day you'll understand.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: No!
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Peter protested.
You'll never wash my feet!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied,
If I don't wash you, you have no part with me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then, Lord, wash not just my feet but my hands and my head as well!
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Simon Peter exclaimed.
Jesus responded, {
Those who have had a bath only need to wash their feet and then they're clean all over. You are clean—but not all of you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For he knew who was betraying him. That's why he said, {
Not all of you are clean.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: After Jesus had washed their feet, he put his robe back on, and sat down. {
Do you understand what I've done to you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: he asked them.
You call me ‘Teacher’ and ‘Lord,’ and rightly so, for that's who I am.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So if I, your Teacher and Lord, washed your feet, you ought to wash one another's feet.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I have set you an example, so you should do just as I did.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you the truth, servants are not more important than their master, and the one sent is not greater than the sender.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Now that you understand these things, you will be blessed if you do them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I'm not talking about all of you—I know those I have chosen. But this is to fulfill Scripture: ‘The one who shares my food has turned against me.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I'm telling you this now, before it happens, so when it does happen you will be convinced that I am who I am.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you the truth, whoever welcomes anyone I send welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me, welcomes the one who sent me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: After he had said this, Jesus was deeply troubled, and declared: {
I tell you the truth, one of you is going to betray me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The disciples looked at each other, wondering which of them Jesus was talking about.
The disciple whom Jesus loved was sitting next to Jesus at the table, leaning close to him.
Simon Peter motioned to him to ask Jesus which one he was talking about.
So he leaned over to Jesus and asked, {
Lord, who is it?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Jesus replied, {
It's the one to whom I will give a piece of bread after I have dipped it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: After dipping the piece of bread, he gave it to Judas, son of Simon Iscariot. Once Judas had taken the bread, Satan entered him. {
What you're going to do, do it quickly,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus told him.
No one at the table understood what Jesus meant by this.
Since Judas was in charge of the money, some of them thought that Jesus was telling him to go and buy what was needed for the Passover festival, or to donate something to the poor.
Judas left immediately after he'd taken the piece of bread, and went out into the night.
After he'd left, Jesus said, {
Now the Son of man is glorified, and through him God is glorified.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If God is glorified through him, then God will glorify the Son himself, and will glorify him immediately.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: My children, I will be with you only a little while longer. You will look for me, but I'm telling you now just as I told the Jews: you cannot come where I'm going.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I am giving you a new command: Love one another. Love one another in the same way I have loved you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you love one another you will prove to everyone that you are my disciples.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Simon Peter asked him, {
Where are you going, Lord?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus answered,
You can't follow me now where I'm going. You will follow me later.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Lord, why can't I follow you now?
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Peter asked.
I'll lay down my life for you.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Are you really ready to die for me? I tell you the truth: before the cock crows you will deny me three times,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
